verdict_reasoning: ATG9A is a multi-pass transmembrane protein (4 TM helices; the only TM protein of the core autophagy machinery) that resides predominantly in intracellular compartments: trans-Golgi network, recycling endosomes, early endosomes, phagophore assembly sites, and autophagosomes. Its dominant compartment is therefore endomembrane. However, multiple independent lines of evidence confirm a genuine plasma-membrane pool: (1) AP-2 sorting-signal mutants accumulate at the plasma membrane (cell-surface biotinylation + immunogold-EM), (2) ATG9A is recycled into the TGN from the plasma membrane via clathrin-coated structures under basal conditions, (3) a published PubMed study explicitly states 'ATG9A displays a broad intracellular distribution and is present in numerous compartments, including the plasma membrane,' and (4) plasma-membrane damage induces rapid translocation of ATG9A to the PM (TIRF, surface biotinylation) where it recruits ESCRT machinery for membrane repair. The topology of the TM domain (multi-pass, cytoplasmic N/C termini, extracellular-facing loops present) is consistent with outer-leaflet loop accessibility. The PM pool is real but minority and partially inducible, placing the verdict at contextual/dual_localization rather than yes.
